DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Attorney, Ryan Gille (415-273-6506), for cross-defendants/demurring parties, DANIEL BURNHAM COURT MASTERS OWNERS ASSOCIATION, DANIEL BURNHAM COURT HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION
Attorney, David L. Rencher (415-877-4486), for defendant/opposing party, Nestor Borillo

CROSS-DEFENDANTS DANIEL BURNHAM COURT MASTERS OWNERS ASSOCIATION and DANIEL BURNHAM COURT HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is argued by both sides. Upon conclusion of arguments, the Court vacates its tentative ruling. The Court rules as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANTS DANIEL BURNHAM COURT MASTERS OWNERS ASSOCIATION and DANIEL BURNHAM COURT HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT - Cross-defendants HOA and MOA's demurrer to the 5th, 6th, 7th, 8th and 9th causes of action in the first amended cross-complaint of Nestor Borillo is overruled. The demurrer as to the 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th and 10th causes of action in the first amended cross-complaint of Nestor Borillo is sustained with 20 days leave to amend to allege facts in good faith as to equitable and extrinsic fraud claims. The first, second, third, fourth and tenth causes of action are barred by the applicable statute of limitations for those claims. Because the HOA and MOA had not been parties to this lawsuit prior to the expiration of the limitations periods, there is no relation back to the filing of the initial complaint by plaintiff TDIC. Nor is there any basis for the application of equitable tolling. The demurrer to the fifth, sixth, seventh, eighth and ninth causes of action is based on the argument that paragraph 6.9 of the CC&Rs precludes any subrogation rights of Dr. Fong's insurer TDIC. While the court takes judicial notice of the CC&Rs, it cannot determine as a matter of law that paragraph 6.9 Rs precludes any subrogation rights of Dr. Fong's insurer TDIC. Tat is not an issue appropriate for resolution on a demurrer, especially since it is far from clear whether a tenant such as Dr. Fong is bound by the CC&Rs and paragraph 6.9 refers to a "named insured" and there is no allegation in the first amended cross-complaint or any fact that the court can take judicial notice of that that phrase includes a tenant. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (D302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment By Defendant Nestor Borillo
Off Calendar Apr-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael K. Pazdernik of Adamst Corzine telephone number 916-983-3900 for The Dentists Insurance Company.
D. L. Rencher of Rencher Law Group telephone number 415-877-4486 for Defendant Nestor Borrillo.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT NESTOR BORILLO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There are triable issues whether the negligence claim of plaintiff The Dentists Insurance Company (TDIC) against Mr. Borillo is barred by the subrogation waiver in the lease or the Daniel Burnham Court Declaration Establishing Reciprocal Easements and Covenants Running with the Land. There is also a triable issue whether TDIC's claim is barred by the indemnity clause in the lease. Section 23 of the lease does not refer to an owner of another unit in the building such as Mr. Borillo as a party for whom the subrogation waiver applies. Section 6.2.4 of the Declaration does not refer to a tenant such as Dr. Fong as a party who is required to waive subrogation. The term "Indemnified Parties" in the Lease does not refer to an owner of another unit in the building such as Mr. Borillo. While it is possible that extrinsic evidence may show that the Lease and/or Declaration bar TDIC's claim, Mr. Borillo has provided no such extrinsic evidence and the court cannot say as a matter of law that the language relied on by Mr. Borillo bars TDIC's claim. As a matter of law, per Civil Code 1714(a), as the owner of an adjacent unit to the unit leased by Dr. Fong, Mr. Borillo owed Dr. Fong a duty not to act unreasonably with regard to his unit which causes harm to Dr. Fong's unit. Moreover, the Conditions, Covenants, and Restrictions impose a general duty on owners making them responsible for maintaining their units so that the units do not deteriorate or present a hazard to or diminish the value and attractiveness of other units. (TDIC Exhibit 7 page 37, section 5.4.1). There are triable issues whether Mr. Borillo breached this duty and whether his alleged breach caused harm for which TDIC may seek damages. (TDIC's responses to Mr. Borillo's Facts 24-25 and TDIC's Additional Facts 16-19). The court did not consider any arguments made for the first time in the reply brief or any evidence submitted with the reply papers. Order signed in open Court. Objections to the declaration of Gino Gonzalez and the deposition testimony of Mr. Miall were overruled on the record.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT By Cross-Defendant Daniel Burnham Court, Llc
Off Calendar Feb-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. D.L. RENCHER, ESQ. of the Rencher Law Group, P.C., Tel No. 415-877-4486 appearing for Cross-Complainant Nestor Borillo;
Atty. ANTHONY G. BENTIVEGNA, ESQ. of Hugo Parker, LLP, Tel No. 415-808-0300 appearing for Cross-Defendant Daniel Burnham.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT DANIEL BURNHAM COURT, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. DBC satisfied its initial burden by presenting the factually devoid discovery responses of cross-complainant Nestor Borillo and Borillo failed to present any evidence creating a triable issue. Borillo's request that the motion be denied or continued per CCP 437c(h) lacks merit because Borillo does not state what facts may exist that could defeat the motion. The Court is not considering Cross-Complainant's supplemental evidence presented at today's hearing (though, Cross-Complainant may have that supplemental evidence filed), finding it not allowed under CCP Sec. 437(C). The Court denies Cross-Complainant's request that the motion be continued. Cross-Complainant would need to file a motion for reconsideration. Cross-Defendant shall submit a proposed order to the Court for signature, which may be e-mailed to the Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
